notes: v329 — IN TRAINING MODE, HOVER ANYTHING ON THE REPORT CARD TO LEARN IT. Following on from the deep-dive and tooltips, this makes the report card fully self-teaching. Every part now carries an explanation: each flag's NAME (a plain-English line on what 🚀 Breakout, 🕵️ Informed?, 🏦 Inst?, ⤴ MA cross, 📉 Dist?, 🥇 Vol record and 🔷 Block day actually mean), the VERDICT ('beat the market' / 'worked as a warning' / two-way), and the 🧩 Combinations, ⛓ Sequences, 🪜 ladder and 📢 announcement headers — on top of the numbers and badges from before. And when you're in training mode, hovering any of them now pops up the app's own clear explanation card (not the plain browser tooltip), so a learner can wander over the whole report and have every name, number, badge, verdict, header and ladder rung explained on the spot. Outside training mode the same explanations still show as normal hover tips. (Next: the same treatment folded into the Lessons.) ALSO RECENT — v328 — FIXED A FREEZE WHEN OPENING THE BOT SETTINGS. A tester hit slow/freezing again. The cause was a line I added last version: the bot's ⚙ settings showed 'Now leading: …' (today's top ladder signals), and building that line quietly re-ran the WHOLE signal grade every time you opened the settings if it hadn't been computed yet — about a second on a big market, several on a phone, which looked like a freeze. Fixed: that line now just reads the grade if it's already been worked out (from opening your 📋 report card, or the bot's own daily run) and otherwise shows a gentle 'open your report card once today' note — it never does the heavy work just to draw the settings. Opening the bot settings is instant again (measured ~1.2s → ~2ms). Nothing about how the bot chooses changed. ALSO RECENT — v327 — THE REPORT CARD NOW EXPLAINS ITSELF. You asked for a deep explanation of the Signal report card and for hovering anything to explain it — both are in. At the top of the card there's a new "📖 How to read this card — a plain-English deep dive" you can open (it remembers your choice): it walks through what the card is, what EDGE really means, the SOLID/PROMISING/TOO-EARLY badges, the hit-rate, median and win/loss sizes, the ✓/✗ both-halves check, the 🟩/🟥 market-tape split, the 📜 persistence record, the difference between singles, combinations and sequences, and the 🪜 ladder including its warning flags. And now you can hover — or press-and-hold on a phone — ANY number, badge or ladder rung and a one-line explanation pops up: the tier badges, EDGE, the fired count, median, wins/losses, the halves tick, the tape, the 📜, and each ladder rung (which also tells you whether it's a buy-side flag or a warning). Same honest maths underneath — just far easier to learn. (The lessons themselves get a matching refresh next.) ALSO RECENT — v326 — 🪜 THE BOT CAN NOW AUTO-FOLLOW THE LADDER. You asked if auto-buy can use the top-rated signals automatically — now it can. New toggle in ⚙ Advanced bot settings (and the 🏆 one-tap setup turns it on): 🪜 Auto-follow my report-card ladder. When on, each day the bot ignores the manual signal ticks and buys only shares firing the top PROVEN signals from your ladder — the ones the card grades SOLID or PROMISING with a positive edge, never an unproven TOO-EARLY one — and its 🧩 combo gate follows the card's best pairs too. It re-reads the ladder every data day, so as flags prove themselves or fade, the bot follows automatically — no re-ticking. It's bullish only (the bot buys), and honest by design: it only ever auto-picks flags the card has actually graded as working, and until a single flag qualifies it leans on the combo gate's proven starter pairs. The 📜 decision diary names the signals it chose each day. The panel shows 'Now leading: …' so you can see today's picks at a glance. ALSO RECENT — v325 — TWO QUICK TESTER FIXES. (1) The ladder now names the signal. The 🥇/🔷 split rungs used to read just "down-day", which didn't say WHICH flag and clashed with the 🥇 rank medal — confusing. They now read "🥇 Vol record · down day" / "🔷 Block day · up day", the rank is a plain gold/silver/bronze number, and the bearish flags (📉 Dist? and the down-day forms) carry a "· warning (predicts a fall)" tag so a green top-ranked WARNING is never mistaken for a buy — it just means that warning has been reading the falls correctly. (2) Announcements now line up with the price history. A tester saw news on a day with no price row, on every share — because the announcements list reached back weeks while the report's price table only showed the last ~10 sessions. Now the report's Recent sessions stretch back far enough to cover the announcements listed, so each 📢 sits on its own price row; anything older than that is noted with the 🏛 link. Announcement dates are also now read exactly as the ASX reports them (no timezone round-trip that could bump a pre-market lodgement to the day before). ALSO RECENT — v324 — 🪜 THE LADDER: a best-to-worst leaderboard at the end of the Signal report card. A tester asked to see, at a glance, which signals are winning right now — so the card now ends with two ranked ladders. ① Singles ranks the individual flags (Breakout, Informed?, Inst?, MA cross, Dist? and the up-day/down-day volume & block splits); ② Combinations & sequences ranks the pairs, triples and accumulation→ignition sequences (top 12). Everything is ranked by its EDGE in its own direction — the bearish flags (📉 Dist?, 🔴 down-day) score by how far price FELL, so a warning that's actually working ranks high and shows green. Each rung has a proportional bar, the edge, a 🥇🥈🥉 for the top three, and the SOLID/PROMISING/TOO EARLY badge — so a big edge that hasn't earned trust yet is still clearly flagged. It re-uses the same honest maths the rest of the card is built from — no new numbers, just a ranked view. As always: one good week proves nothing; the 📜 record across cards is what matters. ALSO RECENT — v323 — SEE WHICH HISTORY DAYS HAD AN ANNOUNCEMENT. Straight after v322: the day-by-day history now marks the days an ASX announcement was lodged with a 📢, so you can tell at a glance whether a volume spike lined up with news. It's on both the in-app 📊 share-detail history (it loads that share's announcements when you open it) and the printable report's Recent sessions table. Hover the 📢 for the headline; the tooltip flags ⚠ when the announcement was market-sensitive. It matches on the exact date, using the same real ASX feed as the list. One honest note: the feed only returns the latest ~20 announcements, so only recent days can be marked — a day without a 📢 isn't a promise nothing was lodged (the caption says so), and the 🏛 Company announcements link always has the full official history. ASX shares only. ALSO RECENT — v322 — COMPANY ANNOUNCEMENTS, NOW ON THE REPORT ITSELF. A tester asked: when you tap Company announcements a new tab opens — it'd be better to see the list of announcements with their dates right on the same page as the price/volume history. Done. The app already pulls REAL announcements from the official ASX feed (it uses them for the news flags and the report card's news-vs-quiet split) — it just wasn't showing you the list. Now the one-share report includes a 'Recent ASX announcements' table right under the recent-sessions history: each announcement's date and headline, with the market-sensitive ones marked ⚠. It loads that share's list automatically when you open the report (instant if it's already been checked today, a few seconds the first time for a new share). (Older notes live in the Owner’s Manual changelog.)
